new_classes.label_en: Semi-sparkling wine pressure quality (≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar at 20 °C)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality of a wine whose excess pressure due to carbon dioxide in solution is not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar, measured at a temperature of 20 °C, OR which is put up in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings — and which is not a sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10.

new_classes.label_en: Bottle with mushroom stopper held by ties or fastenings
new_classes.definition_en: Packaging consisting of a bottle closed with a mushroom-shaped stopper (bouchon champignon) that is held in place by ties or fastenings, as specified for semi-sparkling wine presentations under CN subheadings 2204 21 06 to 2204 21 09.

new_classes.label_en: Semi-sparkling wine other than that of subheading 2204 10, in containers ≤ 2 l — other (CN 2204 21 09)
new_classes.label_de: Perlwein, ausgenommen Wein der Unterposition 2204 10, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger — anderer (KN 2204 21 09)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes (including fortified wines), other than sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10, presented in containers of 2 litres or less, which has an excess pressure due to carbon dioxide in solution of not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar measured at 20 °C or is put up in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings, and which does not fall in subheading 2204 21 06 (i.e. not a wine with a protected designation of origin or protected geographical indication qualifying for that subheading).

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main discriminating criteria for CN 2204 21 09: (1) wine of fresh grapes (FreshGrapeOriginQuality), (2) presented in containers of 2 litres or less (SmallContainer via BFO has_part), and (3) semi-sparkling character — either excess CO₂ pressure ≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar at 20 °C (SemiSparklingWineQuality with decimalRange restrictions on hasExcessPressureAtTwentyDegrees) or bottles with mushroom stoppers (MushroomStopperBottlePackaging class introduced). The complement restriction on SparklingWineQuality encodes exclusion of subheading 2204 10. A coverage gap (reducing score to 0.8) arises because the further sub-distinction within 22042106–22042109 (which separates wines with/without protected designation of origin or geographical indication) is not fully formalised here — a PDO/PGI quality class would be needed to discriminate 22042106 from 22042109, and this is not yet in the TBox. The mushroom-stopper presentation alternative is introduced as a new packaging class but cannot yet be linked as a disjunctive sufficient condition in standard OWL without punning or SWRL, so it is described in the class definition rather than as a formal restriction (minor expressivity gap).
